title: WEF Global Competitiveness Index — Historical Harmonized Dataset (2007-2017), via World Bank Data360 (dataset WEF_GCIHH)
source_org: World Economic Forum (data harmonized/hosted by World Bank Data360, formerly TCdata360)
time_coverage: 2007-2017 for most countries; Iran (IRN) specifically 2010-2017 only (WEF did not survey/rank Iran in the 2007-2009 GCI editions)
frequency: annual (WEF Global Competitiveness Report editions, published Sept each year, discontinued after the 2017-2018 edition when WEF switched methodology to GCI 4.0, which is not part of this harmonized historical series)
units: mixed — WEF_GCIHH_GCI and pillar sub-indices given as both index score (COMP_BREAKDOWN_1='IX', 1-7 scale) and global RANK (COMP_BREAKDOWN_1='RANK', integer); component indicators (EOSQ### = Executive Opinion Survey question scores 1-7, plus hard-data indicators like CORPTAXRATE %, ENROL3GR % tertiary enrollment, BRIBEIDX, CCRATING 0-100 credit rating, AIRSEATKM, FIXEDTELPC, EXPGDP % exports of GDP)
license_terms: World Bank Data360 platform terms (CC BY 4.0 open data); underlying survey content originally WEF Global Competitiveness Report copyright
notes:
GCI (classic methodology, GCI 1.0-3.0) was discontinued by WEF after the 2017-2018 report when they switched to GCI 4.0 (a substantially different indicator set, not covered here) and then paused the index entirely from 2020. This dataset is the World Bank's harmonized re-hosting of the full historical WEF_GCIHH series (2007-2017) on the Data360 platform (the same platform used for wbes-country-profiles), found via web search after the original WEF publications-archive and Kaggle mirror leads did not offer a clean bulk CSV. The Data360 REST API required no Cloudflare bypass or Wayback fallback.

Each file contains the full pillar hierarchy: overall GCI_GCI score+rank; sub-index GCI_A (basic requirements: institutions, infrastructure, macro environment, health/education), GCI_B (efficiency enhancers: goods market, labor market, financial market, tech readiness, market size), GCI_C (innovation and sophistication factors); 12 pillars (numbered 01-12) and dozens of individual component indicators/survey-question scores beneath them, each carrying both an index value and a global rank where applicable (COMP_BREAKDOWN_1 = 'IX' vs 'RANK').

Iran coverage starts in 2010 (not 2007) — WEF's GCI report did not include Iran in its first three editions (2007-08, 2008-09, 2009-10); Iran was added starting with the 2010-11 report. Iran's overall GCI rank/score trajectory as retrieved: 2010 rank 69 (score 4.14) -> 2011 rank 62 (4.26) -> 2012 rank 66 (4.22) -> 2013 rank 82 (4.07) -> 2014 rank 83 (4.03) -> 2015 rank 74 (4.09) -> 2016 rank 76 (4.12) -> 2017 rank 69 (4.27); this trajectory (dip in 2013-14, likely sanctions-era, partial recovery after JCPOA in 2015-17) is directionally consistent with independently known WEF GCI history for Iran, which was used as a lightweight sanity check on data authenticity (no fabrication).

Note WEF_GCIHH is a DIFFERENT/separate Data360 dataset from WB_ES (wb-enterprise-surveys); both happen to be hosted on the same data360api.worldbank.org platform with the same pagination pattern (DATABASE_ID + REF_AREA + top/skip), which is why the same download method was reused across both source slugs in this session.
